title: Apex Fusion Relay Helm Values
description: Supernode-opinionated Apex Fusion relay chart values. This schema is the public configuration interface for LLMs and MCP clients; pass values in this shape directly to Helm.

nameOverride.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Overrides the Kubernetes app name used by the chart.

fullnameOverride.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Overrides the full Kubernetes resource name. Leave empty so the release name controls resource names.

displayName.description: Optional. Human-readable workload name shown by Supernode tooling. MCP normally sets this from releaseName.

image.description: Optional. Apex Fusion node container image settings. Most installs only change image.tag when pinning a specific release.

repository.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Container image repository for the Apex Fusion compatible node image.

tag.description: Optional. Node image tag to deploy.

resources.description: Optional. Kubernetes CPU and memory requests and limits for the relay container. Start with 500m CPU requested, 2 CPU limit, and 4Gi memory for test networks; increase memory for larger networks.

service.description: Optional. Kubernetes Service settings. Keep ClusterIP for in-cluster access; use LoadBalancer only when external node-to-node access is required.

n2nPort.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Node-to-node Service port.

n2cPort.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Node-to-client Service port exposed through the proxy sidecar.

metricsPort.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Metrics Service port.

persistence.description: Required. Persistent storage for the Apex Fusion chain database. Persistence is always enabled for this chart.

storageClass.description: Required. Kubernetes StorageClass used for the chain-data PVC.

accessModes.description: Advanced, usually not modified. PVC access modes.

size.description: Optional. Requested chain database PVC size.

existingClaim.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Existing PVC name to mount instead of creating a StatefulSet volumeClaimTemplate.

node.description: Required. Apex Fusion relay runtime settings. Set node.network and usually leave ports and topology defaults unchanged.

replicas.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Number of relay pods.

network.description: Required. Apex Fusion network to join.

networkMagic.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Overrides derived testnet magic. vector-testnet derives 1 and prime-testnet derives 3311.

n2nPort.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Internal node-to-node port.

metricsPort.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Internal node metrics port.

socketPath.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Node socket path shared with the proxy sidecar.

proxy.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Node-to-client TCP proxy settings. The proxy is always enabled and uses chart-owned nginx configuration.

n2cPort.description: Internal proxy listener port for node-to-client traffic.

nodeSelector.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Kubernetes nodeSelector for relay scheduling.

tolerations.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Kubernetes tolerations for relay scheduling.

affinity.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Kubernetes affinity rules for relay pod placement.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Advanced, usually not modified. Kubernetes topology spread constraints.

topology.description: Optional. Relay topology configuration. Leave mode=image-default for normal public relay behavior. Use relay-service to peer with specific in-cluster relay Services or custom for explicit topology.json roots.

relayTargets.description: Internal relay services to use when mode=relay-service.

useLedgerAfterSlot.description: Topology useLedgerAfterSlot value. Public relays typically use 0.
